questions.question_text: Under Florida law, a radon gas disclosure statement must be included in which real estate documents?
questions.correct_answer: Sale contracts and rental agreements for any building
distractors: Only contracts for homes built before 1978
Only if radon has been detected
Residential contracts in high-risk counties only
questions.explanation: Under s. 404.056(5), F.S., the radon warning must appear on at least one document executed at or before a contract for the sale and purchase of any building, or at execution of a rental agreement for any building — regardless of whether radon has ever been detected. Vacant-land contracts are not covered, and rentals of 45 days or less are exempt.

questions.question_text: A seller knows their property has experienced sinkhole activity. Under Florida law, the seller must:
distractors: Obtain a sinkhole engineer's report first
Only disclose if the buyer asks directly
File a report with the county
questions.explanation: Florida property owners must disclose known sinkhole activity to potential buyers. Sinkholes are common in central Florida, and failure to disclose is a material misrepresentation.

questions.question_text: Under Florida law, property insurers must offer what type of coverage to residential property owners?
questions.explanation: Florida law requires property insurers to offer sinkhole coverage to homeowners. Sinkholes are common in central Florida, and property owners must also disclose known sinkhole activity to potential buyers.

questions.question_text: What makes Florida's radon gas disclosure requirement unique compared to most other states?
questions.correct_answer: The warning is required even when no radon has been detected
distractors: Florida has the highest radon levels nationally
Only licensed inspectors may test for radon
Radon must be below 4 pCi/L to sell
questions.explanation: Florida requires the statutory radon warning in every contract for the sale and purchase of any building and in rental agreements for any building, even if radon has never been tested or detected (s. 404.056(5), F.S.). Most states require disclosure only when radon is known to be present.

questions.question_text: A seller knows about a significant foundation crack but does not mention it in the listing. This is a failure to disclose:
questions.explanation: Material defects that affect property value must be disclosed by the seller, even in an as-is sale. A significant foundation crack affects both value and safety and is considered a material defect.

questions.question_text: When a property is subject to a mandatory homeowners' association, the buyer must receive what document?
distractors: A complete copy of all HOA bylaws
A letter of approval from the HOA board
questions.explanation: When property is subject to a mandatory HOA, s. 720.401, F.S., requires the buyer to receive a disclosure summary before executing the contract. If it is not provided before signing, the buyer may void the contract by delivering written notice within 3 days after receiving the summary or before closing, whichever occurs first.

questions.question_text: A property located within a Community Development District (CDD) requires which special disclosure?
questions.correct_answer: A CDD assessment notice in the contract
questions.explanation: Properties in a CDD require a special assessment disclosure in the contract. CDD assessments appear on the property tax bill and fund infrastructure in new developments, similar to Mello-Roos in California.

questions.question_text: Which disclosure is intended to inform buyers that property taxes may increase after purchasing a Florida property?
questions.explanation: Florida requires a property tax disclosure to inform buyers that property taxes may increase after purchase. This is especially important because the seller's homestead exemption and Save Our Homes cap do not transfer to the buyer.

questions.question_text: Under Florida law, a buyer of real property with a building on it must be notified of what energy-related option?
questions.correct_answer: The option to obtain an energy-efficiency rating on the building
distractors: A mandatory home energy audit before closing
questions.explanation: Section 553.996, F.S., requires that a purchaser of real property with a building be given information, at or before signing the sale contract, about the option of obtaining an energy-efficiency rating on the building. The information is prepared by building energy-efficiency rating system providers — it is no longer a DBPR brochure.

questions.question_text: A seller is aware of an unpermitted room addition that violates building code. Under Florida law, this must be:
distractors: Disclosed only if the buyer's inspector finds it
questions.explanation: Known building code violations must be disclosed to potential buyers. While the seller isn't required to fix the violation before selling, they must inform buyers of its existence.
